retry_reason: If the node changed during the operation (eb != root->node), the function retries; the caller should always retry until success because the root node may be updated asynchronously.
contract_summary: Acquires a read-locked reference to the current root node of a btrfs filesystem. The function repeatedly fetches the root node (via btrfs_root_node, which increments the reference count), takes a read lock, and verifies the node hasn't been replaced. If the node changed, it unlocks and releases the reference, then retries. On success, returns a pointer to the extent_buffer with an elevated refcount and a read lock held. The caller is responsible for releasing the read lock via btrfs_tree_read_unlock and subsequently freeing the reference via free_extent_buffer. This wrapper ensures the caller always obtains a consistent snapshot of the root node under the read lock.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a locked reference to the root node; refcount ops (get via btrfs_root_node, put via free_extent_buffer) are essential but combined with read-locking and consistency check.
judge.retry_reason: If the node changed during the operation (eb != root->node), the function retries; the caller should always retry until success because the root node may be updated asynchronously.

check.reason: btrfs_root_node is called, which returns 'eb' with an incremented reference count (get operation). The access path eb.refs.refs and type chain extent_buffer--refcount_t--atomic_t match the callee information. The function returns this eb to the caller, so the get on return is consistent with the code.
